test.subtitle: 8 questions. 5 possible profiles. Discover what type of rescued animal fits your personality and lifestyle.
test.title: Which pet goes with you?
test.question: Question

questions.q1: How would you describe your daily physical activity level?
questions.q1_a1: High — I exercise or go for walks every day
questions.q1_a2: Moderate — I go out, but I'm not into intense routines
questions.q1_a3: Low — I prefer quiet activities at home
questions.q1_a4: Variable — sometimes a lot, sometimes a little
questions.q2: How many hours are you away from home on an average day?
questions.q2_a1: Less than 4 hours — I'm almost always at home
questions.q2_a3: More than 8 hours — I work long hours away
questions.q3: What type of space do you have at home?
questions.q3_a1: Spacious house with garden or yard
questions.q3_a2: House without garden but with good interior space
questions.q4: What type of bond are you looking for with your pet?
questions.q4_a1: An inseparable companion who's always with me
questions.q4_a2: Close presence but that respects my space
questions.q4_a3: Someone playful and entertaining that makes me laugh
questions.q4_a4: A whole animal family — the more the better
questions.q5: Do you have previous experience with pets?
questions.q5_a1: Yes, I grew up with dogs and know how to care for them well
questions.q5_a4: Lots of experience with multiple types of animals
questions.q6: Are there young children or elderly people in your home?
questions.q6_a4: I live alone or with a partner without children
questions.q7: What monthly budget could you allocate to your pet?
questions.q8: Which of these words best describes you?
questions.q8_a1: Adventurous — I love exploring and moving around
questions.q8_a2: Calm — I value peace and routine
questions.q8_a3: Independent — I need my own space
questions.q8_a4: Empathic — I have a lot of love to give

active_dog.title: The Adventurous Companion
active_dog.tagline: You are energetic, social, and need a companion that keeps up with your pace.
active_dog.description: Your dynamic lifestyle and love for physical activity make you the ideal companion for a medium or large dog. You need a pet that shares your energy, enjoys long walks, and motivates you to go out every day.
active_dog.whyRescued: Adult rescue dogs have already passed the destructive puppy stage. They are loyal, grateful, and adapt quickly to an active home.
active_dog.space: House with yard or near park
tips: Look for breeds or mixes with high energy: border collie, labrador, active mixed breeds.
An adult dog (2–5 years) already has their character defined — you'll know exactly who you're living with.

calm_dog.title: The Calm Friend
calm_dog.tagline: You value close companionship, routine, and quiet moments at home.
calm_dog.description: You prefer a pet that shares your moments of calm, is a constant companion at home, and doesn't require two hours of intense exercise daily.
calm_dog.whyRescued: Many small dogs in shelters were simply abandoned due to their owners' life changes. They are completely domesticated and are looking for exactly what you offer: a stable home.
tips: Look for adult dogs with calm temperament at shelters.
Senior dogs (7+ years) are especially calm and often the most difficult to adopt.

independent_cat.title: The Independent Soul
independent_cat.tagline: You respect autonomy, have a busy life, and want companionship without dependence.
independent_cat.description: Your busy lifestyle and appreciation for personal space make the adult cat your ideal companion.
independent_cat.whyRescued: Adult cats in shelters are the most difficult to adopt. They are completely independent, have already passed the scratching stage, and deeply appreciate whoever gives them a second chance.
tips: An adult cat (2–7 years) already has their character defined.
Two cats keep each other company if you work long hours.

playful_cat.title: The Playful Spirit
playful_cat.tagline: You are creative, curious, and want a pet with personality and character.
playful_cat.description: You like interaction, enjoy spontaneous moments, and value a pet with lots of personality.
playful_cat.whyRescued: Young cats in shelters often come in pairs or groups. Adopting two at once is more economical in terms of mutual entertainment and emotional well-being of both.
tips: Consider adopting two kittens together — they keep each other company and calm each other.
Environmental enrichment (scratching posts, towers, toys) is key for active cats.

multi_pet.title: The Generous Heart
multi_pet.tagline: You have space, time, and enough love for more than one animal.
multi_pet.description: You have the emotional capacity, space, and stability to give a home to more than one pet.
multi_pet.whyRescued: In shelters there are many animals that came together and suffer when separated. Adopting a pair or an already coexisting group is one of the most significant acts of animal welfare.
tips: Talk to the shelter about pairs or groups that already live together.
Make sure you have veterinary resources for multiple pets before adopting.

emergency.title: Emergency guide
emergency.subtitle: Learn how to safely help animals in risky situations.

1.safetyNote: DO NOT approach immediately. A scared or injured animal may bite or scratch out of survival instinct, even if they are normally tame.
1.tipContent: Low tail + curled body = fear. High tail + raised fur = alert/aggression. Lying still = possible serious injury. Growling or barking as you approach = don't get close.

2.safetyNote: If the animal is on the road, signal the area to avoid accidents. Your road safety matters too.
2.tipContent: Use cones, bright colored clothes or any visible object to mark the area. A call to 1551 (Municipalidad) can send support to control traffic.

3.safetyNote: If the animal seems calm, you can attempt first contact. Never do it face-on or abruptly.
3.tipContent: Don't shout its name or make loud noises. Don't chase it if it runs. Don't lift it from the ground suddenly. Don't look directly into its eyes if it's scared.

4.tag: If you decide to move it
4.safetyNote: Use gloves if you have them. If not, use thick clothing, a towel or any fabric to protect yourself.
4.tipContent: A cardboard box with holes, a shopping basket, or the back seat of a car with someone holding it are valid options. The important thing is to keep it calm and safe.

5.safetyNote: Even if the animal seems fine, a veterinary check is essential. Internal wounds, fractures or infections may not be visible.
5.tipContent: That's okay! Contact HuellaViva with the GPS location of the animal. Our team can coordinate the rescue. Your call may be enough to save its life.

6.safetyNote: Rescuing doesn't necessarily mean adopting it. There are many ways to help without taking it home.
6.tipContent: Finding a temporary home for a few weeks can be the difference between life and death for a rescued animal. You don't need to adopt it to save it.

actions.distance: Keep at least 3–4 meters of initial distance
actions.posture: Observe its posture: ears back, low or raised tail indicate fear or aggression
actions.wounds: Check for visible wounds, difficulty moving or signs of illness
actions.location: Note the exact location with GPS or street reference
actions.hazardLights: If on a highway, turn on your vehicle's hazard lights
actions.traffic: Ask someone to direct traffic if necessary
actions.fracture: Don't move the animal if you suspect spinal fracture (fall from height, hit by car)
actions.children: Keep children and uninvolved people away from the immediate area
actions.crouch: Crouch to its level — it seems less threatening than standing
actions.eyeContact: Avoid sustained direct eye contact — it can be interpreted as a threat
actions.backOfHand: Extend the back of your hand (never the palm) about 30 cm and wait for it to approach
actions.tone: Speak in a low, calm tone, without sudden movements
actions.food: If you have food, place it on the ground at a short distance, don't offer it in your hand
actions.gloves: Use work gloves, kitchen gloves or wrap your hands with thick clothing
actions.leash: For dogs: slide an improvised leash (rope, belt) with a soft loop around the neck
actions.towel: For cats: wrap it in a towel or jacket, covering the head first
actions.hold: Hold the animal close to your body so it feels contained and safe
actions.box: Place it in a box, basket or vehicle in a quiet, ventilated area
actions.callAhead: Call HuellaViva or a veterinary clinic before arriving so they can prepare
actions.bite: Report if the animal bit or scratched someone — it may require rabies protocol
actions.meds: Don't give human medications (ibuprofen, acetaminophen are toxic to animals)
actions.immobilize: If it can't move, try not to flex or twist its body — keep it flat
actions.photos: Take photos of the animal's condition and the location where you found it
actions.foster: Temporary home: provide shelter while finding a permanent adopter
actions.social: Post it on social networks with photos and location to find owner or adopter
actions.donate: Donate to a shelter like HuellaViva if you can't care for it
actions.vetCosts: Support veterinary costs even if you can't adopt it
actions.volunteer: Become a volunteer for future cases

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: At HuellaViva Guatemala, we are committed to protecting your privacy. This policy describes how we collect, use and protect your personal information.

collect.title: Information we collect
collect.content: We collect information you voluntarily provide when contacting us for donations, animal reports, or subscribing to our newsletter.

use.title: How we use your information
use.content: We use your information to respond to your inquiries, process donations, coordinate animal rescues and send updates about our work.

protect.title: Data protection
protect.content: We implement adequate security measures to protect your personal information against unauthorized access, alteration or disclosure.

contact.title: Contact
contact.content: If you have questions about this privacy policy, contact us at info@huellaviva.gt

terms.title: Terms and Conditions
terms.intro: Welcome to HuellaViva Guatemala. By using our website, you accept the following terms and conditions.

use.title: Use of the site
use.content: The content of this site is solely informational and educational. We do not guarantee the accuracy or completeness of the content.

donations.title: Donations
donations.content: Donations made to HuellaViva are final and non-refundable, except under exceptional circumstances at our discretion.

liability.title: Liability
liability.content: HuellaViva is not responsible for any damages resulting from the use of information on this site or from interactions with rescued animals.

contact.title: Contact
contact.content: For questions about these terms, contact us at info@huellaviva.gt
